root_text.sn36.23:1.1: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn36.23:1.2: “katamā nu kho, bhante, vedanā, katamo vedanāsamudayo, katamā vedanāsamudayagāminī paṭipadā?
root_text.sn36.23:1.4: Ko vedanāya assādo, ko ādīnavo, kiṁ nissaraṇan”ti?
root_text.sn36.23:2.2: sukhā vedanā, dukkhā vedanā, adukkhamasukhā vedanā.
root_text.sn36.23:2.7: Ayameva ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo vedanānirodhagāminī paṭipadā, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.sn36.23:2.9: Yaṁ vedanaṁ paṭicca uppajjati sukhaṁ somanassaṁ, ayaṁ vedanāya assādo;
root_text.sn36.23:2.10: yaṁ vedanā aniccā dukkhā vipariṇāmadhammā, ayaṁ vedanāya ādīnavo;
root_text.sn36.23:2.11: yo vedanāya chandarāgavinayo chandarāgappahānaṁ, idaṁ vedanāya nissaraṇan”ti.

translation_text.sn36.23:0.2: 3. The Explanation of the Hundred and Eight
translation_text.sn36.23:1.1: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn36.23:1.2: “Sir, what is feeling? What’s the origin of feeling? What’s the practice that leads to the origin of feeling?
translation_text.sn36.23:1.3: What’s the cessation of feeling? What’s the practice that leads to the cessation of feeling?
translation_text.sn36.23:1.4: And what is feeling’s gratification, drawback, and escape?”
translation_text.sn36.23:2.5: Craving is the practice that leads to the origin of feeling.
translation_text.sn36.23:2.7: The practice that leads to the cessation of feelings is simply this noble eightfold path, that is:
translation_text.sn36.23:2.8: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.
translation_text.sn36.23:2.9: The pleasure and happiness that arise from feeling: this is its gratification.
translation_text.sn36.23:2.10: That feeling is impermanent, suffering, and perishable: this is its drawback.
translation_text.sn36.23:2.11: Removing and giving up desire and greed for feeling: this is its escape.”
